seoTitle: How to Make All Images the Same Size in Google Docs
metaDescription: Standardize image sizes in Google Docs using the Size & Rotation panel and locked aspect ratios for a balanced, symmetrical document layout.
intro: Mismatched image dimensions can make an otherwise clean document look messy. This guide covers using the Image options panel to view exact width and height values, locking aspect ratio to avoid distortion, and applying the same measurements across multiple images so everything lines up consistently in reports, catalogs, or presentations.

steps.heading: Select an embedded image
steps.body: Click the photo or graphic you want to resize inside your document to bring up the image editing options.

steps.heading: Open Image options
steps.body: Access the Image options panel from the toolbar or right-click menu to reach detailed sizing controls.

steps.heading: Check Size & Rotation values
steps.body: Open the Size & Rotation section to view the exact width and height dimensions of your selected image.

steps.heading: Lock the aspect ratio
steps.body: Enable Lock aspect ratio so resizing scales the image proportionally without stretching or distorting the subject.

steps.heading: Apply the same dimensions to other images
steps.body: Copy the exact inch or centimeter values and apply them to your remaining images to standardize the scale across the document.

steps.heading: Fine-tune wrapping and alignment
steps.body: Adjust text-wrapping and alignment settings so your uniformly sized images stay lined up cleanly across columns or grids.

faq.question: Will locking aspect ratio crop my images?
faq.answer: No, locking aspect ratio only prevents stretching; it scales the image proportionally rather than cropping any part of it.

faq.question: Can I resize multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Google Docs doesn't offer true batch resizing, so you'll need to apply the same width and height values individually to each image.

faq.question: What's the easiest way to match sizes exactly?
faq.answer: Note the exact dimensions from your first image's Size & Rotation panel, then manually enter those same values for each additional image.

faq.question: Does image size affect document load time?
faq.answer: Very large source files can slow down loading regardless of displayed size, so compressing images beforehand can help with performance.
